response.justification: The candidate is an experienced full-stack software engineer with a web and product focus, but the role demands principal-level hardware performance architecture. There is no evidence of SoC or IP modeling, AI accelerator or GPU architecture work, deep-learning workload analysis, or hardware-software co-design. His low-latency DLang services and brief C/C++ IDE work at National Instruments show performance sensibility in software, but that is far from the silicon-level modeling and architecture feedback this position requires.
strengths: 15+ years of broad software engineering experience, meeting the BS + 8 years bar (ETH Zürich CS)
Low-latency, high-throughput systems work at Dunnhumby/Sociomantic (async I/O, fibers, caching at millions of requests/sec)
Some systems-level C/C++ and embedded-adjacent exposure (National Instruments, robotic arms)
concerns: No SoC/IP modeling, system-on-chip design, or hardware-software co-design experience
No deep neural network training/inference, AI accelerator, or GPU architecture background
Career trajectory is web/full-stack and developer tooling, misaligned with a principal hardware performance IC role

key_factors.factor: No SoC/IP modeling or hardware co-design experience
No AI/deep-learning workload or GPU architecture background
